I. Bonds

(a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) Only three
(d) All the four

III. Stocks
II. Hedge Funds

options_combined: (a) Only one (b) Only two (c) Only three (d) All the four

IV. Lending to private companies
V. Printing and distributing currency notes

(a) I and II only
(b) II, III and IV

raw_blocks_combined:
Which of the following are the sources of income for the Reserve Bank of India?
1. Buying and selling Government bonds
II. Buying and selling foreign currency
III. Pension fund management
IV. Lending to private companies
V. Printing and distributing currency notes
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
(a) I and II only
(b) II, III and IV
(c) I, III, IV and V
(d) I, II and V
options_combined: (a) I and II only (b) II, III and IV (c) I, III, IV and V (d) I, II and V
answer_explanation: RBI earns income from interest on government bonds, foreign exchange operations, and seigniorage from printing currency. It does not lend to private companies or derive significant income from pension fund management. Thus, I, II, and V are correct.

raw_blocks_combined:
